title: paspalum brevifolium var. propinquum
intro: Paspalum brevifolium var. propinquum, also known as Paspalum, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to various parts of the world, including tropical and subtropical regions, and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties. Paspalum is known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and promote wound healing. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used in herbal remedies to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
